26-Point Inspection

Everything We Check & Do in Your Tune-Up

Our comprehensive tune-up covers every moving part of your garage door system — springs, cables, rollers, tracks, opener, sensors, and more.

1

Spring Inspection

Check torsion and extension springs for wear, rust, and proper tension. Flag springs nearing end of life.

2

Cable Check

Inspect both lifting cables for fraying, rust, or uneven wear — catching a failing cable before it snaps saves you an emergency call.

3

Roller Inspection

Check all rollers for cracks, flat spots, or worn bearings. Recommend replacement if needed for smooth, quiet operation.

4

Track Alignment

Inspect both vertical and horizontal tracks for bends, gaps, and proper alignment. Adjust track mounting if needed.

5

Full Lubrication

Lubricate springs, rollers, hinges, tracks, and opener drive mechanism with professional-grade lubricant — not WD-40.

6

Balance Test

Disconnect the opener and manually lift the door to mid-height. A balanced door holds still — an imbalanced door signals spring issues.

7

Hinge & Bracket Check

Inspect all hinges, bottom brackets, and center stiles for cracks, bends, or loose hardware. Tighten all fasteners.

8

Opener Force Test

Test opener force limits and travel limits. Adjust if the door reverses too soon, doesn't close fully, or applies too much force.

9

Safety Sensor Test

Verify both sensors are aligned, LEDs are solid, and the auto-reverse safety function works correctly.

10

Auto-Reverse Safety Check

Test the mechanical auto-reverse by placing a 2x4 under the door. The door must reverse on contact — a federal safety requirement.

11

Weather Seal Inspection

Check bottom seal and side seals for cracks, gaps, or deterioration that allows water, pests, or drafts to enter.

12

Full Report & Recommendations

We provide a written summary of your door's condition and any issues found — with honest recommendations and no pressure.

When to Schedule

Signs Your Garage Door Is Due for a Tune-Up

Most homeowners never schedule maintenance until something breaks. These are the signs your door is telling you it needs attention now.

Squeaking or Grinding Noise

Noisy operation means metal-on-metal contact somewhere — usually dry rollers, hinges, or tracks. A tune-up eliminates noise before it causes damage.

Door Moves Slowly or Unevenly

Sluggish or jerky movement signals worn rollers, friction buildup, or spring tension issues — all caught and corrected during a tune-up.

Door Is More Than 1 Year Old

Annual maintenance is the single best investment for your garage door. Most manufacturers recommend a yearly tune-up to maintain the warranty and prevent premature failure.

Opener Straining or Running Loud

If your opener sounds more labored than usual, the door system — not the opener — is likely the cause. Lubrication and adjustment can fix this immediately.

Recently Moved In

Don't know the history of your garage door? A tune-up gives you a full condition report and catches any deferred maintenance left by the previous owner.

Preparing to Sell Your Home

A properly maintained, quiet garage door makes a strong impression. A tune-up before listing ensures your door won't fail during a showing or inspection.

Why SolidRise

Why Bay Area Homeowners Choose Us for Maintenance

True 26-Point Inspection

We don't just lubricate and leave. Our tune-up covers every component — springs, cables, rollers, tracks, opener, sensors, seals, and more.

Honest Written Report

We give you a written summary of everything we found — no hidden upsells. If something needs attention, we show you and explain the options.

Professional-Grade Lubricants

We use lithium-based garage door lubricant — not WD-40. The right lubricant lasts longer, attracts less dirt, and doesn't damage rubber seals.

Catches Problems Early

A spring or cable caught during maintenance costs a fraction of what it costs after it snaps. Prevention is always cheaper than emergency repair.

Bilingual Service — Español

Nuestro equipo habla español. We explain everything we find and recommend in your preferred language — clearly, no pressure.
